videos.title: The reason 2026 is exactly the same as the situation just before the 1990 recession: The US credi...
videos.subtitle_summary:
1. The video discusses the possibility of a repeat of the 1990 recession due to global credit tightening, with high difficulty in obtaining loans and rising energy costs causing concern. This situation is referred to as "credit crunch."
2. The 1990 recession was caused by the collapse of a savings and loan (S&L) industry in the United States, which had taken out risky loans using real estate as collateral. When the real estate market crashed in the late 1980s, many S&L institutions went bankrupt, causing losses for those who invested in them.
3. In response to this crisis, international banking regulations known as Basel I were introduced to prevent similar problems by requiring banks to maintain a higher ratio of pure capital to total assets (or loans). This means that banks should lend less and keep more safe loans while reducing risky ones. The credit crunch situation is caused by this tightening of credit. In August 1990, the Gulf War led to a sharp increase in oil prices, making it necessary for companies that consume a lot of energy to borrow more money. This situation was exacerbated as the costs of raw materials and transportation increased, leading to higher overall debt for companies. The crisis was particularly evident in the airline industry, with companies like Pan Am and Midway Airlines going bankrupt due to increased fuel prices and reduced travel demand during the Gulf War. The collapse of the S&L industry also affected the US automobile market, as consumers shifted from gas-guzzling SUVs and trucks to more fuel-efficient small and foreign cars. This shift led to a decline in sales for domestic car manufacturers. Additionally, chemical companies that produce plastic and synthetic chemicals faced declining profits due to rising prices of raw materials.

videos.title: 20,000 Civil Servants Face ‘Pension Gap’… The Start of the Debate on Extending the Retirement Age
videos.subtitle_summary: 20,000 civil servants are facing a 'pension gap' due to discrepancies between retirement age and pension eligibility. Currently, civil servants retire at 60 but receive pensions from 62 onwards. However, changes are being proposed to delay pension eligibility until 64 in 2027, 65 in 2030, and 66 in 2033. This could potentially affect up to 20,000 civil servants, raising concerns about their financial stability and quality of life post-retirement. Some argue that extending the retirement age is necessary due to increased lifespans, while others question if it's fair for civil servants, who have traditionally relied on pensions as a stable source of income. The government may be considering these changes due to the financial pressures of an aging society.

videos.title: Exchange rate breaches '1,500 won' during trading... First time since financial crisis / SBS 8 News
videos.subtitle_summary:
1. The exchange rate of the Korean won against the dollar exceeded 1,500 won for the first time since the financial crisis in 2009, due to a surge in international currency rates. This increase raises concerns about a potential shock to our economy.
2. Today's exchange rate started at 1,500.3 won, surpassing the rate from last Friday by 7.3 won. Although it briefly reached 1,500 won during recent night trading, today's rate marks the first time since March 2009 that it has exceeded this level during daytime trading. The rate closed at 1497.5 won after a slight drop, but still set a new high record since November 2008.
3. The rise in the dollar price is attributed to increased uncertainty in international markets due to Iran's oil embargo and foreign investors selling 8.4 trillion won worth of stocks today, further pushing up the exchange rate. This strong depreciation of the Korean won compared to major currencies could negatively impact our export competitiveness, increase production costs, and potentially harm domestic businesses.

videos.title: [Stock Recommendation] The true No. 1 company in nuclear power technology surpassing Doosan Enerb...
videos.subtitle_summary:
1. 2026년 후반기, 원전(Nuclear Power Technology) 주제로 큰 수익을 볼 수 있는 이유를 설명합니다. 원전은 국가 및 세계 규모의 프로젝트로 10년 이상 지속될 것으로 예상됩니다.
2. 현재, 국내 원전 산업은 정부 정책과 투자 자금을 통해서 글로벌 수요가 지속적으로 늘어나고 있습니다. 특히, 소형원전 SMR(Small Modular Reactor)은 차세대 에너지라는 주요 섹터로 보아야 합니다.
3. 최근 뉴스들을 보면, 한국형 소형 모듈 원전이 미국 시장으로 진입할 수 있다는 가능성을 내놓고 있습니다. 정부와 글로벌 기업들은 SMR 시장의 본격화를 알리고 있으며, 미국은 중국 다음으로 SMR 상용화를 추진하는 국가입니다.

videos.title: 유가·환율 불안에 15조 원 추경…이번 주 시장 전망은?  [세상만사] #유가환율 #추가경정예산안 #중동사태 #국제유가 #채상미교수
videos.subtitle_summary:

2. 중도 사태 이후 지금 하락폭을 보면, 주요 증시 중에서 우리나라 코스피가 많이 빠지고 있습니다. 이는 한국의 경제 구조적 취약성을 노출한 결과로 보여집니다.
3. 이번 사태로 인해 에너지 의존도가 굉장히 높은 이제 경제 구조를 가지고 있으며, 외국인이 한국 주식을 우선 매도하고 있습니다. 또한 환율이 지속적으로 약세를 보이고 있고, 유가 비용이 증가한 이론 악순환 고리를 보이고 있습니다.

videos.title: [공모주] 메쥬&한패스 1일차 결과 & 2일차 예상 - NH스팩 수요예측 결과와 금액 배분은?
videos.subtitle_summary:
1. 공모주 매주와 한패스의 1차 청약이 마감되었습니다. 신한 투자 증권이 매주의 주관사로, 청약권수는 158,851건으로 총 증거금은 0.34조원입니다. 한국사 증권과 대신 증권이 한패스의 주관사로, 청약권수는 각각 186,278건과 2499건으로 총 증거금은 아직 비례 청약하시는 분들이 대기 중인 것으로 보입니다.
2. 2일차가 시작되면 균등은 빠르게 줄어들 것으로 예상합니다. 매주는 9.5조 5조 가량의 증거금으로 비례 한 주에 5,651만 원이나 필요할 것으로 보며, 한 패스는 약 2.6조 가량의 증거금을 예상하며 비례 한 주당 약 1,900만 원 정도가 필요합니다.
3. 일반 등급 풀차역에는 각각 비례 한 주에서 두 추첨으로 갈라질 것으로 예상하며, 가성비는 해당 단위의 소수점보다 높게 잡아야 안전하다고 볼 수 있겠습니다.
